tax: Pall Choke-Wind is placed at species rank beneath Choke-Wind (Aeolian order, Air Elementals class). Diagnosis rests on a heavy foul-air body, silent suffocation, and flame-guttering as its only visible sign. It is separated from its sibling the Gust Whirler by its slow settling foul still air versus the Whirler's fast spinning gust; the two share the genus body-plan but never the spinning motion. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: heavy oxygen-poor foul gas held coherent in low ground.
ap: Pall Choke-Wind stands indefinite, a pooling body filling a low gallery. Its silhouette is an invisible heavy pool, betrayed only by guttering flame. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: lamps and torches that dim, gutter, and die for no draught; a faint foul or sweetish reek in still low ground; and a sense of breathlessness and heavy limbs before the danger is understood. It moves with a slow gravity-fed flow into the lowest ground, following prey downhill, and shows none; invisible, betrayed by dying flame. Even after dispersal it can be identified from a low gallery where flames will not hold and small bones of suffocated vermin collect, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
eco: Pall Choke-Wind persists in low still galleries, sumps' air-pockets, and dead-end dips where heavy foul gas can pool and persist. It does not breed; it forms where air-pressure animates a pocket of heavy foul gas seeping from rock or rot, and recurs whenever the seep refills the low ground with foul air and the body re-coheres. It sustains its mass on air-pressure in heavy foul gas, drawing in foul gas drawn from seep and rot to maintain its body. Range is bounded by the low ground it can pool in: outside that band the body disperses and breaks up where draught reaches it. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as a low dip where a test-flame dims without any draught to explain it, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Pall Choke-Wind is roused by a breathing crew descending into its low ground and otherwise lies a still invisible pool in a quiet dip. It claims a single low gallery or dip and answers intrusion with flowing over a descending crew to suffocate silently. Toward grey-folk it is the deadliest because invisible; survey crews report that Lanternkin carry a low test-flame into every dip, since a dying flame is the only warning. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
traitsText: Stable characters of Pall Choke-Wind: a body of heavy oxygen-poor foul gas held coherent in low ground; immune to all physical attack, being only foul air; and a defining weakness — ventilation and strong draught, which scatter it. Its senses run through the medium itself: air-motion sensing; it follows the breath and warmth of prey. It tolerates darkness, heat, and cold but fails at ventilation and strong draught. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, the low-pooling body and flame-guttering sign, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
abilities: Abilities of Pall Choke-Wind are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it suffocates silently by displacing breathable air; it follows prey downhill into low ground; it draws foul gas from seep and rot to renew itself; and it pools invisibly, betrayed only by guttering flame. Each is a direct expression of a coherent body of heavy oxygen-poor gas, so it cannot, for instance, produce heat, spin, strike with force, or persist in moving air. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
bg: In the Throat, Pall Choke-Wind matters because it kills silently in low ground, and a low test-flame carried into every dip is now standard practice. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as a suffocation hazard logged with low still galleries, and its presence reshapes routes through the low dips and dead-ends, now entered only behind a test-flame and ventilation. A test-flame that dims in a windless dip is the Lanternkin's one warning of a Choke-Wind.
g: Pall Choke-Wind (Aer mortifer) is an air elemental of foul settled gas that pools in low galleries and suffocates without a sound. Documented from the low, still galleries where heavy foul air collects, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a coherent body of heavy, oxygen-poor foul air pooling in low ground — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. It is the still, deadly sibling of the Whirler: not motion but the absence of breathable air, given just enough will to follow prey down. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a guttering of every flame and a slow return of breathable draught and no carcass.
